en:
A boy made a strange bet that he could swallow many eggs.
His friends laughed and thought he was only joking.
To everyone's surprise, he swallowed thirteen raw eggs one by one.
Then he finished with a boiled egg as if it were easy.
People called him a record-holder for the day.
His teacher warned that the bravest stunt is not always the wisest one.
cn:
一个男孩打了个奇怪的赌，说自己能吞下很多鸡蛋。
朋友们大笑，都以为他只是开玩笑。
出乎所有人意料，他真的一个接一个吞下了十三个生鸡蛋。
随后他还若无其事地吃下一个煮鸡蛋收尾。
那一天，人们把他叫作“纪录保持者”。
老师提醒大家，最大胆的举动并不一定最明智。

en:
A famous actress decided to stay out of the limelight for a while.
She joined a small theatre and accepted the role of a servant.
She wore plain clothes and stood behind the main actors.
Most of the audience did not recognize her at all.
After the show, staff discovered who she really was.
She said she only wanted the joy of acting without constant attention.
cn:
一位著名女演员决定暂时远离公众焦点。
她进入一家小剧场，接下了仆人角色。
她穿着朴素，总是站在主角身后。
大多数观众完全没有认出她。
演出结束后，工作人员才发现她真正的身份。
她说自己只想安静演戏，不想总被关注包围。

en:
An oil tanker hit rocks at sea and the situation became critical.
Crew members sent an SOS signal while trying to save the ship.
Radio stations received the message and rescue teams were dispatched.
Some sailors were taken off in time, but oil leaked into the water.
The accident caused serious pollution along the coast.
Later it became a classic lesson in maritime emergency communication.
cn:
一艘油轮在海上触礁，局势迅速变得危急。
船员一边抢险，一边发出了 SOS 求救信号。
无线电台收到信息后，救援力量被紧急调动。
部分水手及时撤离，但石油仍泄入海中。
这次事故在沿海造成了严重污染。
后来它成为海上应急通信的经典案例。

en:
On April Fools' Day, a TV program showed people picking spaghetti from trees.
The presenter spoke so seriously that many viewers believed the story.
Some even called the station to ask how to grow their own spaghetti trees.
The newsroom received far more calls than expected.
Later the staff explained that it had been a holiday joke.
Embarrassed at first, many people finally laughed at the brilliant hoax.
cn:
愚人节那天，一档电视节目播出人们从树上采意大利面的画面。
主持人语气过于严肃，许多观众竟然信以为真。
有人甚至打电话到电视台，询问如何种“面条树”。
编辑部收到的来电数量远超预期。
随后工作人员解释，这只是节日玩笑。
很多人起初有些尴尬，最后还是被这个创意逗笑了。

en:
Archaeologists discovered an ancient mummy and sent it for careful treatment.
Before the operation, the team had prepared every step in detail.
All instruments had been sterilized and checked in advance.
During the procedure, the fragile body was handled with extreme care.
Doctors and researchers recorded each stage patiently.
In the end, the operation succeeded and valuable historical data were preserved.
cn:
考古学家发现一具古老木乃伊后，将其送去精细处理。
手术前，团队已经把每个步骤都详细准备好。
所有器械都提前完成了消毒和检查。
操作过程中，这具脆弱遗体被极其小心地处理。
医生和研究人员耐心记录了每一个阶段。
最终手术成功，宝贵的历史资料也得以保存。

en:
A heavy smoker announced that he was about to smoke his last cigarette.
At first he felt confident and threw away his spare packs.
Soon the old habit returned and tempted him again.
He has given up smoking, but the change has not been easy.
For weeks he has been avoiding smoking areas and late-night parties.
Step by step, he is learning to live without tobacco.
cn:
一位老烟民宣布自己要抽掉最后一支烟。
起初他很有信心，还把备用烟盒全扔了。
不久旧习惯又回来诱惑他。
他已经开始戒烟，但这个改变并不轻松。
几周来，他一直在避开吸烟区和深夜聚会。
他正一步步学会没有烟草的生活。

en:
A traveler flew to Buenos Aires and enjoyed the journey by air.
He watched clouds and coastlines from his window seat.
When it was time to return, he suddenly felt afraid of flying.
Instead of taking another plane, he chose to travel by sea.
The boat was slower, but he felt calmer on board.
His friends were surprised that one trip changed his mind so completely.
cn:
一位旅客曾飞往布宜诺斯艾利斯，享受了空中旅程。
他坐在窗边，看着云层和海岸线掠过。
可到返程时，他突然对飞行感到害怕。
他没有再坐飞机，而是改走海路。
船程更慢，但他在船上反而更安心。
朋友们很惊讶，一次旅程竟让他想法大变。

en:
For the Great Exhibition of 1851, engineers designed the Crystal Palace.
The huge building was made of glass and steel.
Many parts were built in advance and assembled quickly on site.
Visitors from around the world entered and admired its bright interior.
Later the structure was moved and rebuilt in another area.
Although it was eventually destroyed by fire, its influence on modern architecture remained.
cn:
为了 1851 年万国博览会，工程师设计了水晶宫。
这座巨型建筑由玻璃和钢材构成。
许多部件提前制作好，再在现场快速组装。
来自世界各地的参观者走进展馆，赞叹其明亮空间。
后来这座建筑被迁移并在新址重建。
尽管最终毁于火灾，它对现代建筑的影响仍然深远。

en:
During the war, a prisoner escaped from a guarded camp at night.
He did not run far at first but hid in open countryside.
In daytime he stayed behind rocks or walls to avoid detection.
At night he moved across fields towards safer territory.
For several days he survived with little food and no shelter.
At last he reached friendly forces and secured his freedom.
cn:
战争期间，一名囚犯在夜里从看守营地逃脱。
他起初没有跑太远，而是躲在开阔乡野。
白天他藏在岩石后或墙边，避免被发现。
夜里他穿过田野，朝更安全的区域前进。
几天里他几乎没有食物和庇护，却坚持活了下来。
最终他到达友军控制区，重新获得自由。

en:
A fisherman caught a creature unlike any fish he had seen before.
Its rough skin and strange head shocked people on the dock.
Some said it was a sea monster, while others disagreed.
He showed it to a fishmonger, but even the expert hesitated.
Reporters arrived and asked, "It's dangerous, isn't it?"
The argument continued for hours, and no one gave a final answer.
cn:
一名渔夫捕到一种他从未见过的奇怪生物。
它粗糙的皮肤和异样的头部让码头上的人震惊。
有人说它是海怪，也有人坚决反对。
他把它拿给鱼贩看，但连行家也一时犹豫。
记者赶来追问：“它很危险，不是吗？”
争论持续了好几个小时，始终没人给出最终结论。

en:
After the elections, a former prime minister announced his return home.
He had lived in exile abroad for many years.
By the time his plane landed, supporters had gathered outside the airport.
He thanked the crowd and gave a short speech.
Reporters asked what he would do next in politics.
He replied that he would listen to the people before making any decision.
cn:
大选结束后，一位前首相宣布回国。
他已经在海外流亡多年。
在飞机降落前，支持者就已聚集在机场外。
他向人群致谢，并发表了简短讲话。
记者追问他下一步会如何参与政治。
他回答说，在做决定前会先倾听民众意见。

en:
Bus drivers went on strike, and city traffic quickly became chaotic.
Passengers have been waiting at stops since early morning.
Long queues formed, and many commuters arrived late at work.
In the middle of the confusion, an uninvited passenger appeared on a bus.
Officials came to question him and restore order.
The strike has shown how deeply daily life depends on public transport.
cn:
公交司机开始罢工后，城市交通很快陷入混乱。
乘客从清晨起就一直在站台等待。
长队不断出现，许多通勤者上班迟到。
就在混乱中，一名不速之客出现在一辆公交车上。
工作人员赶来询问情况并恢复秩序。
这次罢工已经说明，日常生活对公共交通有多么依赖。
